title: Fix fj-lax eval_unary_elementwise tensor F32 element widening (dtype/element mismatch)
description: Tensor arm at crates/fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs:1272-1276 routes BF16->from_bf16_f32 and F16->from_f16_f32 but the catch-all '_ => Literal::from_f64(mapped)' captures F32 as well. So F32 tensors declare DType::F32 (per line 1262) but their element literals are F64Bits — same invariant violation as 2chb but for F32 in eval_unary_elementwise. Add an explicit DType::F32 arm using from_f32.
notes: Added explicit DType::F32 arm in eval_unary_elementwise tensor element constructor so F32 tensors now contain F32Bits literals matching their declared dtype (was previously producing F64Bits elements in F32-declared tensors). fj-lax 761 + ad_numerical_verification 75 pass, clippy clean.

title: [parity-gap] safe egraph reciprocal-reciprocal erases subnormal overflow
description:
Review evidence:
- Commits 2971d17, 348837a, 2ab703f, and 1ccef8f moved several unsafe egraph identities behind OptimizationConfig::aggressive().
- safe_algebraic_rules still contains reciprocal-reciprocal: (reciprocal (reciprocal ?a)) => ?a.
- That identity is not safe for tiny nonzero floats: 1.0 / 1e-320 overflows to +Inf, then 1.0 / +Inf is +0.0, while the rewrite returns 1e-320.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add a fj-egraph unit test building chained_unary_jaxpr(Primitive::Reciprocal, Primitive::Reciprocal).
2. Evaluate original and optimize_jaxpr_with_config(..., &OptimizationConfig::safe()) with Value::scalar_f64(1e-320).
3. Assert optimized output matches original bit/value semantics.
4. Run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_egraph_recip RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph reciprocal_reciprocal -- --nocapture
5. Current safe optimizer can extract ?a and return 1e-320 instead of the original +0.0.
close_reason: Moved reciprocal-reciprocal to numerically_unsafe_rules; subnormal overflow breaks identity

title: Add F32 Erf/Erfc/ErfInv VJP dtype-preservation + tests
description: Erf/Erfc/ErfInv VJPs use scalar_value() for the 2/sqrt(π) and similar constants — these are F64. For F32 inputs the F64 constant widens the gradient to F64. fj-lax already rejects complex Erf so widening only affects half/float real types. Fix: thread scalar_constant_matching_dtype through all three. Add erf_vjp_numerical_f32 test asserting F32 dtype is preserved.
close_reason: Threaded scalar_constant_matching_dtype through Erf/Erfc/ErfInv VJPs (2/√π, -2/√π, √π/2 constants). fj-lax scalar eval_unary_elementwise still widens non-complex scalars to F64 internally (latent issue beyond scope) so end-to-end F32 dtype preservation needs that follow-up. erf_vjp_numerical_f64_value_sanity pins f64 value correctness. 75 ad_numerical_verification tests pass; clippy clean. Committed ae76379.

title: [code-review] Security gate misses cargo-fuzz manifest targets
description: [HIGH] crates/fj-conformance/src/security_adversarial.rs generated a green security adversarial report from a hard-coded fuzz family list, but crates/fj-conformance/fuzz/Cargo.toml declares additional cargo-fuzz bins not represented in the report. Missing manifest targets such as cache_persistence_format, primitive_eval_fuzzer, and partial_eval_fuzzer meant the gate could claim 100% fuzz coverage while ignoring working harnesses. The gate should validate report.fuzz_families against every [[bin]] target in the fuzz manifest and include those omitted targets with deterministic corpus evidence.

title: Add ConvertElementType primitive for dtype casting

title: Fix fuzzer input-count placeholders for control flow and multi-input primitives
description:
WHAT: Fix the hardcoded arity=1 placeholder for 10 primitives in crates/fj-conformance/fuzz/fuzz_targets/common.rs:257-268. The comment "Default placeholder" confirms these are wrong.
WHERE: The function primitive_input_count() at approximately line 200 of common.rs.
HOW: Set correct input counts based on each primitive definition:
  - DynamicUpdateSlice: 3 (operand, update, start_indices)
  - OneHot: 1 (indices tensor; num_classes is a param)
  - Cumsum: 1 (input tensor; axis is a param)
  - Cumprod: 1 (input tensor; axis is a param)
  - Sort: 1 (input tensor; dimension is a param)
  - Argsort: 1 (input tensor; dimension is a param)
  - Conv: 2 (lhs, rhs; strides/padding are params)
  - Cond: 3+ (predicate, true_operands, false_operands)
  - Scan: 2+ (init_carry, xs)
  - While: 1+ (init_state)
Note: Cond/Scan/While also need sub-jaxpr generation in the fuzzer, which is a harder problem. For this task, at minimum fix the numeric arity. Sub-jaxpr fuzzing can be a follow-up.
WHY: With arity=1, the fuzzer generates malformed inputs for these primitives, meaning they always fail at arity validation and never exercise the actual primitive logic. This is wasted fuzzing cycles.
TESTING: Run the fuzzer briefly to confirm it no longer panics on arity mismatches for these primitives.
ACCEPTANCE: The "Default placeholder" comment is removed. Each primitive has a documented, correct input count.
close_reason: Implemented real primitive arity counts for parameterized conformance fuzz targets; added regression test; verified with rch cargo test -p fj-conformance --all-targets

title: Implement complex pow in apply_complex_binary (fj-lax arithmetic.rs)
description:
WHAT: Add complex exponentiation to apply_complex_binary() in crates/fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs:145-164.
WHERE: The match arm at line 160 currently returns "complex arithmetic not yet implemented" for all binary ops except add/sub/mul/div.
HOW: Complex pow uses the identity (a+bi)^(c+di) = exp((c+di)*ln(a+bi)). Decompose: r=|z|, theta=atan2(b,a), ln(z)=(ln(r), theta), then exp of the product. For integer exponents, use repeated squaring for efficiency.
WHY: JAX supports complex pow natively. Without it, grad(f) fails for any traced program that involves complex exponentiation.
TESTING: Add tests in fj-lax for complex pow at several points: (1+i)^2 = 2i, (1+i)^(0.5), purely real complex pow matches real pow. Add numerical AD verification for complex pow VJP.
ACCEPTANCE: The "complex arithmetic not yet implemented" error no longer triggers for Primitive::Pow with complex inputs.
close_reason: Completed: implemented complex pow in fj-lax with integer fast path and direct evaluator tests; rch cargo test -p fj-lax passed

title: Conv panics on zero stride parameter
description:
[HIGH] crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_ops.rs accepts strides=0 for Primitive::Conv and then performs division by that stride instead of returning a typed EvalError. In 1D conv, eval_conv_1d parses params["strides"] at lines 2817-2820 and then uses width.div_ceil(stride) for SAME padding at line 2824 or (width - kernel_w) / stride for VALID padding at line 2835. In 2D conv, parse_stride_pair returns zero at lines 2976-2986 and compute_output_and_pad divides by stride at lines 2997 and 3005.

Repro: construct lhs TensorValue::new(DType::F32, Shape { dims: vec![1, 3, 1] }, three F32 literals) and rhs TensorValue::new(DType::F32, Shape { dims: vec![1, 1, 1] }, one F32 literal), set params { "strides": "0", "padding": "valid" }, then call eval_primitive(Primitive::Conv, &[lhs, rhs], &params). Current debug/test builds panic on division by zero; release builds can also compute invalid output geometry.

Expected: reject zero or malformed strides before arithmetic with a typed EvalError::Unsupported such as "conv stride must be positive". Add regression tests for 1D VALID, 1D SAME, and 2D zero-stride inputs.
close_reason: Rejected zero and malformed Conv strides before output geometry arithmetic

title: Fuse partially-overlapping Rev∘Rev pairs in egraph shape-chain prepass
description: optimize_shape_parametric_chains currently cancels Rev∘Rev only when both equations specify the exact same axis set (after sort/dedup via rev_axes). Two consecutive Rev equations on a single-use intermediate with axis sets A and B that overlap partially are equivalent to one Rev with axis set (A ⊕ B) — symmetric difference — because each axis in A ∩ B is reversed twice (no-op) while each axis in A ⊕ B is reversed exactly once. Acceptance: (a) extend fuses_shape_chain with (Rev, Rev) gated on a non-empty symmetric difference and add a sym-diff path in the fuse branch that rewrites current's axes param to the deduplicated sorted symmetric difference; (b) the existing cancellation branch already handles A == B, so the new fuse path only fires for partial overlap; (c) add unit tests for partial overlap (rank-3 [0,1] then [1,2] -> [0,2]) and for the empty-overlap case ([0] then [1] -> [0,1]); (d) workspace tests + clippy clean via rch.
close_reason: Implemented Rev∘Rev partial-overlap fusion: new rev_axes_symmetric_difference returns Some(sym-diff) iff both sides parse; extended fuses_shape_chain with (Rev, Rev) gated on non-empty sym-diff (cancellation branch handles A==B case); fuse branch rewrites current's axes param to sym-diff CSV. Added 2 unit tests (rank-3 partial overlap [0,1]+[1,2]->[0,2] and disjoint [0]+[1]->[0,1]) with eval-equivalence assertions. Verified: fj-egraph 134 tests, conformance preserves_semantics 33 tests, clippy clean. Committed 036a25a.

title: fj-cache FileCache::path_for trusts CacheKey::as_string for filename — add path-traversal guard
description: crates/fj-cache/src/backend.rs:132 FileCache::path_for builds a cache file path via self.cache_dir.join(format!('{}.bin', key.as_string())). Because CacheKey is a public struct with pub fields and as_string returns 'namespace-digest_hex', a maliciously-constructed key with digest_hex containing path separators or '..' could traverse outside cache_dir on write or read. The canonical build_cache_key always produces hex digests, but defense-in-depth at the boundary prevents future regressions if a caller hand-rolls a key. Acceptance: (a) add a free helper fn 'fn is_safe_cache_filename(s: &str) -> bool' that returns true iff every char is ASCII alphanumeric, '-', '_', or '.'; (b) in FileCache::get/put/evict, reject keys whose as_string() fails this check by either returning None / not persisting / counting a put_failure or evict_failure as appropriate; (c) add a unit test that constructs a CacheKey with digest_hex='../foo' and asserts: put silently rolls a put_failure, get returns None, evict returns false without touching disk; (d) verify the normal hex-digest path still passes via existing round-trip tests; (e) workspace tests + clippy clean.
close_reason: Added is_safe_cache_filename allowlist (ASCII alphanumeric + '-_.'). FileCache::get returns None, put bumps put_failures, evict returns false for unsafe keys. 3 new tests cover positive cases, separator/control-byte/non-ASCII rejection, and end-to-end put/get/evict refusal on a '../etc/passwd'-style key. fj-cache 90 tests pass; clippy clean. Committed 763ba10.

title: Cumsum/Cumprod panic on empty tensors

For a valid empty tensor on the selected axis, e.g. TensorValue::new(DType::I64, Shape { dims: vec![0] }, vec![]), axis_dim == 0 and total == 0, so Primitive::Cumsum/Primitive::Cumprod panic with division by zero. Repro: call eval_primitive(Primitive::Cumsum, &[input], &BTreeMap::new()); the same applies to Cumprod and axis=-1. Expected JAX/NumPy behavior is to return an empty tensor with the same shape/dtype. Add regression tests for shape [0] and a zero-sized selected axis such as [2,0] axis 1, then return early when axis_dim == 0.

title: [code-review] Safe egraph reciprocal involution changes subnormal behavior
description:
Review finding from commits 1ccef8f/2ab703f in crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs.

Defect: `safe_algebraic_rules()` still includes `reciprocal-reciprocal`; default `optimize_jaxpr()` now uses `OptimizationConfig::safe()`, so the safe/default optimizer rewrites `reciprocal(reciprocal(x))` to `x`. That is not safe at subnormal boundaries. For f64 `x = 1e-320`, the original program computes `1.0 / (1.0 / x)`: the inner reciprocal overflows to +Inf and the outer reciprocal returns +0.0. The optimized program returns 1e-320. This violates the config docs that safe mode protects denormals and violates JAX/IEEE observable behavior for the unfused operation.

Concrete repro:
1. Add a conformance test in `crates/fj-conformance/tests/egraph_preserves_semantics.rs` that builds `v2 = reciprocal(v1); v3 = reciprocal(v2)`.
2. Evaluate the original Jaxpr and `optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr)` with `Value::scalar_f64(1e-320)`.
3. Expected: safe/default optimization preserves the original +0.0 result, or keeps the reciprocal chain. Actual: optimized output is 1e-320 because the rule removes both reciprocals.
4. Run with `rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-conformance --test egraph_preserves_semantics reciprocal_subnormal -- --nocapture`.

Suggested fix: move `reciprocal-reciprocal` into `numerically_unsafe_rules()` unless a range/type proof is available, and add the conformance test above.
close_reason: Duplicate of frankenjax-elh8, which tracks the same safe egraph reciprocal-reciprocal subnormal parity gap.

Also wired vmap(grad) dispatch path in composition_oracle_parity.rs. All 39 API tests + 4 composition oracle tests pass.

title: Fix large I64 precision loss in sort and argsort
description:
Review finding [HIGH]. In crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_ops.rs, sort_along_axis currently converts integral tensor elements to f64 before sorting (lines around 2589 and 2611-2639). Values above 2^53 cannot be represented exactly as f64, so Sort can silently rewrite distinct I64 literals when it casts the sorted f64 back to i64, and Argsort can compare rounded values instead of the original integer keys.

Reproduction to add as a failing test: evaluate Primitive::Sort on an I64 tensor [9007199254740993, 9007199254740992]. Expected exact sorted values are [9007199254740992, 9007199254740993], but the current implementation routes both values through f64 and can collapse/corrupt the larger literal. Also evaluate Primitive::Argsort on [9007199254740993, 9007199254740992, 9007199254740994] and assert the indices reflect true i64 ordering, not f64-rounded ordering.

Suggested fix: sort integral tensors with an i64 key path that preserves the original Literal for Sort and uses the same exact key for Argsort. Keep the f64 total_cmp path for floating tensors so NaN ordering remains covered by existing tests. Validate with rch cargo test -p fj-lax sort and rch cargo test -p fj-conformance --test sort_oracle.
close_reason: Fixed exact integer sort and argsort ordering with oracle regressions

title: Remove dispatch batching panic surfaces
description: crates/fj-dispatch/src/lib.rs and crates/fj-dispatch/src/batching.rs still contain non-test panic surfaces in transform dispatch and vmap batching paths. title: Add AD rule coverage matrix documentation test

All other 100+ primitives have both VJP and JVP rules. This test prevents silent AD rule gaps. Skill: /porting-to-rust

title: [parity-gap] safe egraph transcendental identities erase NaNs
description: Review finding from recent safe-egraph dispatch work: OptimizationConfig::safe still included identities that are not JAX/IEEE-safe. Repro 1: optimize sin(x)*sin(x)+cos(x)*cos(x) at x=+Inf; original evaluates NaN, rewrite returns 1. Repro 2: optimize cosh(x)*cosh(x)-sinh(x)*sinh(x) at x=1000.0; original observes +Inf - +Inf = NaN, rewrite returns 1. Repro 3: optimize logistic(NaN)+logistic(-NaN); original propagates NaN, rewrite returns 1. Repro 4: optimize max(a, min(a, b)) or min(a, max(a, b)) with a=NaN and b=5.0; current JAX-compatible max/min behavior returns the non-NaN operand, but absorption rewrites return NaN. Move these identities behind numerically_unsafe_rules and add safe/aggressive regression coverage.
close_reason: Completed: moved unsafe exact transcendental and max/min absorption egraph identities out of safe mode, added fj-egraph safe/aggressive NaN-boundary regressions, and preserved public strict egraph dispatch coverage.

title: Implement complex unary ops in eval_unary_elementwise (fj-lax arithmetic.rs)
description:
CONTEXT: eval_unary_elementwise() at fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs has TWO stubs (scalar at ~line 1038, tensor at ~line 1075) returning complex arithmetic not yet implemented when a unary op receives Complex64/Complex128 input.

SCOPE: For ops that JAX supports on complex (sign, square, reciprocal, is_finite): implement the complex logic. For ops JAX does NOT support on complex (floor, ceil, round, lgamma, digamma, erf, erfc, erf_inv): change error to not supported for complex dtype (permanent correct error, not deferred).

CHANGES: 1) Scalar match (line ~1038): dispatch Complex64/Complex128 to per-op complex implementations. 2) Tensor match (line ~1075): same dispatch for tensor elements. 3) Ops unsupported on complex get descriptive permanent error.

TESTING: sign(1+1i)=(1+1i)/sqrt(2), reciprocal(2+0i)=0.5+0i, is_finite(1+2i)=true, floor(complex) returns not supported error. Verify all existing tests pass; log before/after test counts.

ACCEPTANCE: Lines ~1038 and ~1075 no longer contain not yet implemented.
close_reason: Completed: implemented supported complex unary ops and replaced remaining unary complex stubs with permanent descriptive errors; rch cargo test -p fj-lax passed

title: [parity-gap] egraph literal rewrites erase output dtype
description:
Review evidence:
- The egraph language stores bare numeric rewrite constants as FjLang::Num(i64), and id_to_atom lowers every FjLang::Num back to Literal::I64.
- safe_algebraic_rules has literal-producing rewrites such as is-finite-const-0/1 => 1, bitwise-xor-self => 0, pow-zero => 1, and integer-pow-zero => 1.
- Existing tests around these rules mostly assert equation count, not output dtype/value parity after eval.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add a fj-egraph unit test for unary_jaxpr(Primitive::IsFinite, Atom::Lit(Literal::I64(0))).
2. Compare eval_jaxpr(original, &[]) to eval_jaxpr(optimize_jaxpr(&original), &[]).
3. Expected original output is Value::scalar_bool(true); current optimized literal path can produce Literal::I64(1).
4. Add the same check for bitwise_xor(x, x) with a U32/U64 input and pow(f64_x, Literal::I64(0)); both should preserve the reference output dtype.
5. Run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_egraph_dtype RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph egraph_literal_dtype -- --nocapture
close_reason: Moved dtype-erasing rewrites (pow-zero, integer-pow-zero, bitwise-xor-self, is-finite-const-0/1) to numerically_unsafe_rules

title: [code-review] egraph lowering panics on malformed Jaxpr inputs
description: Recent egraph safety commits were reviewed for panic surfaces. jaxpr_to_egraph is public and still indexes malformed graphs directly: Atom::Var(var) lowers via var_map[var] in crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs, and each supported primitive then indexes input_ids[0], input_ids[1], or input_ids[2] without checking equation arity. optimize_supported_segment catches EGraphLoweringError but cannot catch these panics. Repro: construct Jaxpr::new(vec![], vec![], vec![VarId(2)], vec![Equation { primitive: Primitive::Add, inputs: smallvec![Atom::Var(VarId(1)), Atom::Lit(Literal::I64(1))], outputs: smallvec![VarId(2)], .. }]) and call jaxpr_to_egraph or optimize_jaxpr. Expected: typed lowering error such as MissingVariable or ArityMismatch, no panic. Actual: BTreeMap indexing panics before EGraphLoweringError can be returned. Add explicit missing-variable and arity validation plus adversarial malformed-Jaxpr tests.
close_reason: Fixed review findings: egraph lowering now returns typed errors for malformed variables/arity, optimize_jaxpr defaults to safe mode, and conformance coverage opts unsafe rewrites into aggressive mode.

title: Reshape silently wraps oversized new_shape dims
description:
[HIGH] crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_ops.rs eval_reshape parses the new_shape parameter as i64 but casts non-negative dimensions with `as u32` before checking range. This is still present at lines 401-414 for scalar inputs and lines 445-479 for tensor inputs. A requested dimension above u32::MAX silently wraps instead of returning EvalError, which is the same bug class recently fixed for BroadcastInDim/Concatenate/Pad output shapes.

Repro: construct an empty tensor with `TensorValue::new(DType::I64, Shape { dims: vec![0] }, vec![])`, wrap it as `Value::Tensor`, set params `{ "new_shape": "4294967296" }`, and call `eval_primitive(Primitive::Reshape, &[input], &params)`. Current code casts 4294967296 to 0, computes total 0, and can return shape `[0]` instead of rejecting the impossible requested shape. Scalar reshape has the same unchecked cast path and can also mis-handle out-of-range dimensions before validating product.

Expected: reject negative and out-of-u32-range dims before casting, use checked multiplication for known/output products, reject inferred dimensions that exceed u32::MAX, and add regression tests for `new_shape=4294967296` plus product overflow cases that do not allocate.
close_reason: Rejected out-of-u32 reshape dims and checked reshape products before casting

title: Fix fj-ad OneHot/Argsort VJPs returning scalar 0 instead of zeros_like(input)

JAX convention (and the rest of fj-ad — see BroadcastedIota at 2276, BitcastConvertType at 2284) is to return zeros_like(&inputs[0]) so the cotangent's shape and dtype match the original input. Scalar zeros break shape composition in downstream autodiff.

title: Add Reshape∘Reshape fusion to fj-egraph shape-chain prepass
description: In crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs::optimize_shape_parametric_chains, two consecutive Reshape equations on the same value (single-output, single-use intermediate) can be fused into one Reshape with the second's new_shape param. The intermediate Reshape's only effect on the carried value is to update shape metadata; if the next equation also Reshapes the same data, the intermediate shape is unobservable and the fused single Reshape produces the same final shape and elements. Currently cancels_shape_chain only handles Transpose∘Transpose, ExpandDims∘Squeeze, Squeeze∘ExpandDims, and Rev∘Rev — Reshape chains are NOT optimized. Acceptance: (a) extend the prepass with a fuse_shape_chain branch for (Reshape, Reshape) that drops the previous Reshape and keeps current; preserve aliasing semantics like cancellation; (b) the fusion only triggers when previous Reshape's output is single-use; (c) add a unit test in fj-egraph's existing test module verifying the fusion fires; (d) add a metamorphic test in egraph_preserves_semantics.rs showing chained Reshape on a 6-element tensor reshaped 6 → 2x3 → 3x2 produces the same TensorValue as the unoptimized jaxpr; (e) workspace clippy + tests must remain green via rch.
close_reason: Implemented Reshape∘Reshape fusion in optimize_shape_parametric_chains: new fuses_shape_chain helper + fuse branch in the prepass loop that drops single-use intermediate Reshapes and rewires inputs. Added 2 unit tests in fj-egraph (positive fusion + extra-consumer guard) and 2 metamorphic tests in fj-conformance/tests/egraph_preserves_semantics.rs (concrete fusion + inferred-dim path). All fj-egraph (129), fj-conformance::egraph_preserves_semantics (33), fj-dispatch+fj-api (398) tests pass via rch; clippy clean. Committed 2df8fe5.

title: fj-ad scan_vjp force-converts all xs gradients to F64 and silently zeros complex
description: crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs:3707-3736 scan_vjp initializes g_xs_elements with Literal::from_f64(0.0) and then writes each step's xs gradient via 'Literal::from_f64(lit.as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0))' before wrapping in TensorValue::new(DType::F64, ...). Two problems: (1) For real-valued inputs (f32, i64, u32, bf16, f16) this works but loses dtype precision — the scan VJP always returns f64 regardless of xs dtype, breaking downstream type-matching expectations. (2) For Complex64/Complex128 inputs, Literal::as_f64 returns None, so 'unwrap_or(0.0)' silently zeros every element of the xs gradient and the final tensor is misreported as DType::F64 even though the input was complex. Same pattern in the scalar arm (line 3725). Acceptance: (a) preserve the input dtype: declare 'let out_dtype = xs_tensor.dtype' once, use it in the final TensorValue::new; (b) replace the per-step Literal::from_f64(lit.as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0)) writes with direct copies of step_grads[1] elements (cloning the Literal); (c) initialize g_xs_elements with a dtype-appropriate zero literal helper (a small match on xs_tensor.dtype emitting Literal::I64(0), Literal::from_f64(0.0), Literal::Complex64Bits(0, 0), etc.); (d) add a regression unit test that scans add over a Complex64 xs and asserts the resulting xs gradient (a) has dtype Complex64, (b) is not all-zero (use a non-trivial gradient input); (e) workspace tests + clippy clean via rch.
close_reason: scan_vjp now copies step VJP gradients verbatim instead of coercing via as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0). New observed_grad_dtype tracks the body's actual output dtype, propagated as the output TensorValue dtype; mixed-dtype scan bodies error out (defensive). dtype_for_literal helper added. 2 regression tests: f32 preserves dtype/element alignment + Complex64 grad with complex cotangent stays Complex64 AND not all-zero. fj-ad 272 tests + ad_numerical_verification 58 + dispatch_ad_oracle 19 pass; clippy clean. Committed 86497e4.

title: Optimize DFT twiddle factor computation - precompute roots of unity
description:
**Performance issue in fj-lax/src/fft.rs:**

The O(n²) DFT fallback computes sin_cos() for every (j,k) pair:
```rust
for (k, out) in output.iter_mut().enumerate() {
    for (j, &(xr, xi)) in input.iter().enumerate() {
        let angle = -2.0 * PI * (j as f64) * (k as f64) / (n as f64);
        let (sin_a, cos_a) = angle.sin_cos();  // Called n² times!
```

**Fix:** Precompute the n-th roots of unity once (n calls), then lookup by `(j*k) % n`.
This reduces trig calls from O(n²) to O(n) for non-power-of-2 lengths.

**Skill applied:** /profiling-software-performance
close_reason: Precomputed n-th roots of unity in DFT, reducing trig calls from O(n²) to O(n). Commit d0114ff.

title: fj-ad zeros_like and ones_like return F64 for Complex/Bool/U32/U64/BF16/F16 inputs
description: crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs:343-368 and 405-424 — zeros_like and ones_like build a zero/one gradient that mismatches the input dtype for several kinds: Complex64/Complex128 inputs return F64 scalars (should be Complex64Bits(0,0)/Complex128Bits(0,0)); BF16/F16 inputs return F64 (should be BF16/F16); Bool/U32/U64 also widen to F64. The mismatched dtype then propagates through value_add/value_mul, silently widening the entire gradient chain — same root cause class as the FFT VJP widening (frankenjax-4y5q/35ur/gvkt) but more pervasive: zeros_like is used as the initial cotangent for almost every primitive that needs a zero-vector gradient. Acceptance: (a) extend zeros_like to emit dtype-matching zero literals for Complex64 → Complex64Bits(0,0), Complex128 → Complex128Bits(0,0), BF16 → from_bf16_f32(0.0), F16 → from_f16_f32(0.0); (b) keep Bool/U32/U64/I64 returning I64 zeros (matching integer gradient convention) — these are NOT typically differentiable so emitting the existing integer zero is fine, but at least don't silently turn them into F64; (c) same fix for ones_like; (d) reuse the existing zero_literal_for_dtype helper if it covers the cases; (e) add a regression test 'zeros_like_preserves_complex64_dtype' asserting zeros_like of a Complex64 tensor stays Complex64; (f) workspace tests + clippy clean.
close_reason: zeros_like/ones_like now preserve dtype for differentiable kinds (BF16/F16/F32/F64, Complex64/Complex128) — Complex64 inputs get Complex64(0,0)/(1,0) gradients of matching precision instead of being silently widened to F64. KEPT legacy F64-widening for non-differentiable kinds (Bool/U32/U64) since AD convention treats those gradients as F64 zeros (callers like switch_vjp depend on this). I64 stays I64. New one_literal_for_dtype helper. 2 regression tests covering both new behaviour AND the preserved legacy widening to guard against future regressions in either direction. fj-ad 274 + ad_numerical_verification 63 tests pass; clippy clean. Committed eafe422.

notes: trigamma_value (digamma JVP/VJP scalar) now preserves the input dtype via literal_from_f64_for_dtype + dtype_for_literal/tensor.dtype. F32/BF16/F16 digamma derivatives no longer widen to F64. fj-ad 287 + ad_numerical_verification 75 pass, clippy clean.

title: Split panics on zero num_sections for empty axes
description:
`eval_split` accepts `num_sections=0` because `parse_usize_param` allows zero and the Split path never validates that the section count is positive. For an empty split axis, current Rust reports `0usize.is_multiple_of(0) == true`, so `eval_split` reaches `let section_size = axis_size / num_sections` and divides by zero at `crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_ops.rs:3550`.

Reproduction sketch:
1. Build an empty tensor, for example `TensorValue::new(DType::I64, Shape { dims: vec![0] }, vec![])` wrapped as `Value::Tensor`.
2. Call `eval_primitive(Primitive::Split, &[input], &BTreeMap::from([("num_sections".into(), "0".into())]))`.
3. Expected: `Err(EvalError::Unsupported { primitive: Primitive::Split, detail: ... })` because zero sections is invalid.
4. Actual: panic from integer division by zero in `eval_split`.

The recent Split oracle suite in `crates/fj-conformance/tests/split_oracle.rs` covers positive section counts but does not include invalid zero-section coverage. Fix should reject `num_sections == 0` before divisibility/reshape logic and add regression coverage for empty-axis and non-empty-axis inputs.
close_reason: Fixed: added explicit check rejecting num_sections==0 before divisibility logic in eval_split. Added 3 regression tests for zero sections on both empty and non-empty axes.

title: Fix fj-ad jvp_jaxpr out-tangent fallback returning scalar 0 instead of zeros_like(primal)
description: In jvp_jaxpr at crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs:5837-5846, when a tangent is missing from tangent_env for an output variable, we fall back to Value::scalar_f64(0.0). This breaks shape composition for tensor outputs — the consumer expects a tangent matching the primal's shape and dtype. Use zeros_like(primal) instead, looking up the primal from primal_env.

title: [code-review] safe egraph clamp rewrites bypass validation
description:
Review evidence from the six-hour egraph hardening window:
- `crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs` still keeps `clamp-to-minmax` and `clamp-same` in `safe_algebraic_rules` at lines around 431-433.
- `fj-lax` `eval_clamp` rejects complex/bool literals and rejects unsupported scalar/tensor input forms before computing values.
- Safe egraph rewrites can bypass those checks: `clamp(a, a, a) => a`, or `clamp(x, lo, hi) => min(max(x, lo), hi)` can route through broader binary broadcasting semantics instead of clamp's stricter contract.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add a regression that builds `out = Clamp(x, x, x)` with one input `x`.
2. Evaluate the original with `args = [Value::Scalar(Literal::from_complex128(1.0, 0.0))]`. Expected: `eval_jaxpr` returns the existing clamp `TypeMismatch` (`clamp is not supported for complex dtypes`).
3. Evaluate `optimize_jaxpr_with_config(&jaxpr, &OptimizationConfig::safe())` and `optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr)` with the same args. Current safe/default can rewrite to `x` and return the complex value.
4. Add a `clamp-to-minmax` shape/kind case such as scalar `x` with tensor `lo`/`hi`; `eval_clamp` rejects unsupported input forms, while transformed max/min may broadcast and succeed.
5. Run: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_clamp_safe_rules RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph clamp_validation -- --nocapture`.

Suggested fix: move clamp rewrites out of safe mode until egraph carries enough dtype, kind, and shape evidence to prove the original `eval_clamp` validation has already succeeded.
close_reason: Moved clamp validation-skipping egraph rewrites out of safe mode and added validation regression coverage.

title: [reality-check] support function-level custom_vjp/custom_jvp
description:
JAX's @custom_vjp and @custom_jvp decorators work at the function level with fwd/bwd callbacks.

**Gap:** FrankenJAX has primitive-level VJP/JVP registration via register_custom_vjp/register_custom_jvp, but not function-level decorator semantics that work with composed Jaxprs.

**Effort:** Medium - extend AD tracing to recognize custom derivative boundaries.

**Prerequisites:** None (extends existing infrastructure).

**JAX reference:** `jax.custom_vjp`, `jax.custom_jvp`
close_reason: Already implemented: fj-api/src/transforms.rs has custom_vjp() and custom_jvp() functions with fwd/bwd callbacks. Tests pass. Updated gap report to reflect parity_green status.

title: fj-ad cumsum VJP zeros complex gradients via as_f64 round-trip
description: crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs:2175-2243 Cumsum VJP converts gradient tensor through f64 via .as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0).collect::<Vec<f64>>(), does reverse cumsum in f64, then rebuilds with Literal::from_f64 and DType::F64. For Complex64/Complex128 inputs this silently zeros the gradient (since as_f64 returns None for complex literals). For F32/BF16/F16 it widens to F64. Cumsum is a common primitive used in attention and other ML workloads, so this widening propagates broadly. Same root cause as scan_vjp (frankenjax-kpqu) and dynamic_update_slice_vjp (frankenjax-tpu6). Acceptance: (a) preserve gradient dtype by tracking it through the reverse-cumsum directly on Literal values (not f64); (b) for tensors, output dtype = input g_tensor dtype; (c) handle complex by accumulating real and imaginary parts separately for Complex64/Complex128 inputs (each cumsum sums in field arithmetic); (d) regression test with Complex64 input + Complex64 cotangent, assert resulting gradient stays Complex64 and is not all-zero; (e) workspace tests + clippy clean.
close_reason: Cumsum VJP now preserves dtype: complex inputs use (re,im) f64-pair accumulation re-encoded at input's complex dtype, real inputs keep f64 reverse-cumsum but re-encode at input's natural dtype (F32/BF16/F16/F64/I32/I64/U32/U64). Bool falls back to F64 per AD convention. Output TensorValue dtype = input dtype (except Bool→F64). 2 regression tests: Complex64 reverse-cumsum produces correct values AND keeps dtype + F32 input keeps F32. fj-ad 277 tests pass; clippy clean. Committed 9d1e46b.

Acceptance: (a) test mul_vjp_numerical_complex64 builds two Complex64 scalar primals, applies vjp_single with Complex64 cotangent, asserts gradients are Complex64-typed and match the known closed-form mul derivative (d(a*b)/da = b, d(a*b)/db = a in complex arithmetic); (b) workspace tests + clippy clean via rch.
close_reason: Added mul_vjp_numerical_complex64 in ad_numerical_verification: a=2+3i, b=-1+4i, g=0.5-0.25i → g_a=(0.5, 2.25), g_b=(1.75, 1.0) via complex multiplication. Pins Complex64 dtype preservation AND exact value correctness for the simplest Complex64 AD case. Side cleanups: #[allow(dead_code)] on unused take_real_part back-compat alias from 35ur; replaced vec! with array literal in rfft_vjp_numerical_f32 (clippy). ad_numerical_verification 64 tests pass; clippy clean. Committed 26e4a05.

title: Make batched fixture timeouts return without joining timed-out workers
description:
Review finding [HIGH]. Commit 0985d27 changed run_transform_fixture_bundle_batched in crates/fj-conformance/src/lib.rs to record timed-out worker handles, but the function then joins all timed_out_handles before returning (current lines around 1145-1186). That means case_timeout does not bound wall-clock runtime: a timed-out case with a long or hung worker still blocks the whole batched run at the final join.

Reproduction to add: construct a TransformFixtureBundle with one case having simulated_delay_ms = 5000 and BatchRunnerConfig { case_timeout: Duration::from_millis(1) }. Measure elapsed time for run_transform_fixture_bundle_batched. Expected behavior for a timeout runner is to return a Timeout report promptly after roughly the configured timeout; current behavior waits for the delayed worker during the final join and returns only after the worker finishes. A genuinely hung worker would hang the harness indefinitely despite emitting a timeout report.

Suggested fix: preserve bounded fan-out without joining timed-out workers on the critical return path. Options include a scoped worker pool with cooperative cancellation, a persistent bounded runner, or clearly documenting and renaming the timeout as classification-only. Add an elapsed-time regression test so future code cannot reintroduce timeout-as-blocking-join semantics. Validate with rch cargo test -p fj-conformance test_batch_runner.
close_reason: Detached timed-out batch workers from the return path and added elapsed-time regression coverage

Add div_vjp_numerical_complex64 test with explicit Complex64 inputs and the expected complex-division result. Acceptance: (a) test asserts Complex64 dtype on both gradients; (b) gradient values match closed-form within 1e-5 f32 tolerance; (c) workspace tests + clippy clean.

title: Batch runner timeout leaks between test cases
description:
## Problem
The `test_batch_runner_marks_timeout` test is failing because timeout state leaks between test cases.

Test setup:
- Case 1: `batch_timeout` with `simulated_delay_ms: 200`, timeout of 50ms → Expected: Timeout
- Case 2: `batch_fast_after_timeout` with `simulated_delay_ms: 0`, same 50ms timeout → Expected: Pass

Actual behavior: Case 2 is being classified as Timeout instead of Pass.

## Location
`crates/fj-conformance/src/lib.rs:2203` - `test_batch_runner_marks_timeout`

## Root cause
The batch runner likely isn't resetting timeout state between cases, or there's a timing issue with how the timeout check is implemented.

## Fix needed
Review `run_transform_fixture_bundle_batched` to ensure timeout tracking is per-case, not cumulative.

title: Avoid holding LRU order lock during backend eviction
description:
Skill: /deadlock-finder-and-fixer

Verified hazard: crates/fj-cache/src/eviction.rs LruCache::enforce_budget holds the internal order Mutex while invoking CacheBackend::stats and CacheBackend::evict. CacheBackend is a public trait for arbitrary backends, so calling backend code while holding the wrapper lock creates a reentrancy/deadlock hazard if a backend observes or re-enters the LRU wrapper, logs through shared cache state, or blocks behind code that needs the order lock.

Reproduction target: add a custom test backend whose stats/evict methods try_lock the LruCache order mutex. On the current implementation, inserting beyond max_entries or max_bytes fails because enforce_budget still holds order while calling into the backend.

Expected fix: shorten the order critical section, pop eviction keys into local values, drop the lock, and only then call backend stats/evict. Keep LRU ordering behavior unchanged and add a regression test that proves backend calls are made without the order lock held.
close_reason: Completed: moved LRU budget eviction backend calls outside the order mutex critical section and added a reentrancy regression test proving backend stats/evict run without the order lock held.

description: Review finding from the recent safe-egraph dispatch commits: OptimizationConfig::safe still includes factor/distribute and floating associativity rewrites. Repro: optimize a Jaxpr for (x * y) + (x * z) with x=1e308, y=2.0, z=-2.0. JAX/IEEE evaluates the original as +Inf + -Inf = NaN, but factor can rewrite it to x * (y + z) = 0. Move distributive/factor and non-associative floating association rules behind numerically_unsafe_rules, and add safe/aggressive regression coverage.
close_reason: Completed: moved floating associativity/distribution/factor rewrites behind aggressive mode and added overflow-visible NaN regression coverage for safe egraph optimization.

title: UBS: verify lock and async-blocking findings

Initial spot check shows fj-cache lock recovery often uses unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()), which may be an UBS false positive, while fj-trace uses Rc<RefCell> for trace contexts and fj-conformance simulates fixture delay with thread::sleep. Audit these clusters, refactor any real runtime risk, and document or suppress verified false positives.
close_reason: Completed: audited UBS lock/async clusters; hardened fj-ffi RwLock poison handling and added poisoned-registry recovery coverage. Verified fj-cache/fj-ad already recover poisoned locks, fj-trace Rc<RefCell> tracing borrows are single-threaded and short-lived, and fj-conformance simulated delay is synchronous fixture timeout plumbing rather than async blocking.

title: fj-ad Slice VJP zeros complex gradients and widens real inputs to F64
description: crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs:1818-1880 Slice VJP collects gradient elements via .as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0) and rebuilds result_elements with Literal::from_f64. Output dtype is hardcoded F64 (the initial zero tensor uses Literal::from_f64(0.0)). Same dtype-loss + silent-zero pattern as Cumsum/Pad/scan/dynamic_update_slice VJPs. Complex inputs are silently zeroed; F32/BF16/F16 are widened to F64. Acceptance: (a) preserve g_tensor's dtype throughout — initialize result_elements with zero_literal_for_dtype(g_dtype); (b) copy Literal values from g directly into result_elements (no f64 round-trip); (c) output TensorValue with g_dtype, not hardcoded F64; (d) regression test for Complex64 input verifying Slice VJP keeps Complex64 and embeds the gradient at correct positions; (e) workspace tests + clippy clean.
close_reason: Slice VJP now preserves dtype: g_dtype extracted up front via dtype_for_literal/g_tensor.dtype; result_elements initialized with zero_literal_for_dtype; g elements copied as Literals verbatim (no f64 round-trip); output TensorValue uses g_dtype not hardcoded F64. Regression test pins Complex64 Slice VJP correctness with exact value placement. fj-ad 279 tests pass; clippy clean. Committed 64c053b.

title: Conv trace shape inference panics on zero 2D stride
description: [HIGH] crates/fj-trace/src/lib.rs Conv shape inference parses params["strides"] into usize values but does not reject zero in the rank-4 path. Repro: create a SimpleTraceContext with lhs ShapedArray { dtype: F64, shape: [1,3,3,1] } and rhs ShapedArray { dtype: F64, shape: [1,1,1,1] }, then call process_primitive(Primitive::Conv, &[lhs_id, rhs_id], params) with padding="same" and strides="0,1" or "0,0". Expected: Err(TraceError::InvalidPrimitiveParam or ShapeInferenceFailed) matching fj-lax runtime stride validation. Actual: the 2D path assigns stride_h=0 at lines around 1490-1492, then SAME padding reaches height.div_ceil(stride_h) around line 1522 and panics in debug/test builds. The 1D path also uses strides[0].max(1), silently accepting strides="0" instead of rejecting it, so the fix should share strict positive stride parsing across 1D and 2D trace inference and add regression tests for 1D zero stride and 2D zero height/width stride.
close_reason: Fixed: removed .max(1) workaround in 1D, added explicit zero stride validation in both 1D and 2D paths. Added 5 regression tests.

title: [parity-gap] safe egraph pow-two rewrites erase promoted dtypes
description:
Review evidence from the last-six-hours egraph safety commits:
- 14fcdc9 moved add/sub zero, mul/div/pow/integer_pow one, and max/min commutativity/associativity out of safe mode, but safe_algebraic_rules still keeps pow-two and integer-pow-two in crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs.
- Primitive::Pow evaluates through fj-lax binary dtype promotion. For a U32 input and an I64 literal exponent 2, promote_dtype(U32, I64) => I64, so the original Jaxpr returns Value::Scalar(Literal::I64(49)).
- The safe egraph rule pow-two rewrites pow(x, 2) to mul(x, x). Evaluating Mul over two U32 operands returns Value::Scalar(Literal::U32(49)), so safe/default optimization changes the observable dtype.
- Primitive::IntegerPow currently returns F64 for numeric scalar/tensor inputs. The safe egraph rule integer-pow-two rewrites integer_pow(x, exponent=2) to mul(x, x), so a U32 input changes from F64(49.0) to U32(49).

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add cases to numerical_safety_mode_preserves_promoted_dtype_identity_boundaries in crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs for Pow(VarId(1), Literal::I64(2)) and integer_pow_jaxpr(2) with args [Value::scalar_u32(7)].
2. Evaluate the original with eval_jaxpr; expected outputs are I64(49) for Pow and F64(49.0) for IntegerPow under the current fj-lax contracts.
3. Evaluate optimize_jaxpr_with_config(&jaxpr, &OptimizationConfig::safe()) and optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr); current safe/default can emit Mul(var,var) and return U32(49).
4. Run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_pow_two_dtype RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph numerical_safety_mode_preserves_promoted_dtype_identity_boundaries -- --nocapture

Suggested fix: move pow-two and integer-pow-two to numerically_unsafe_rules until egraph lowering can prove and preserve the result dtype, or replace them with typed rewrites that keep the promoted output contract.
close_reason: Moved pow-two and integer-pow-two out of safe mode; added promoted dtype regression coverage.

title: Default vmap reduce_window window dimensions in BatchTrace

title: [parity-gap] reduce_window max/min still skip NaNs
description:
Review evidence:
- Commit 521de15 changed Primitive::Max/Min and ReduceMax/ReduceMin to JAX-style NaN propagation.
- crates/fj-lax/src/lib.rs reduce_window_accumulate_literal still uses f64::max/f64::min via (*value).max(input) and (*value).min(input), so sliding-window max/min keep Rust maxNum/minNum behavior and can return a non-NaN from a window containing NaN.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add a conformance test in crates/fj-conformance/tests/reduce_window_oracle.rs using Primitive::ReduceWindow with params reduce_op=max, window_dimensions=2, window_strides=1, padding=VALID and input Value::vector_f64(&[1.0, f64::NAN, 3.0]).
2. Assert both output windows are NaN, matching JAX lax.reduce_window(..., lax.max).
3. Run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_reduce_window_nan RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-conformance --test reduce_window_oracle reduce_window_nan -- --nocapture
4. Current code returns non-NaN values because reduce_window_accumulate_literal uses .max/.min instead of the JAX NaN-propagating helpers.
close_reason: Fixed reduce_window max/min to use jax_max_f64/jax_min_f64 helpers for NaN propagation. Added 4 conformance tests.

title: Fix fj-ad basis_value_like widening to F64 regardless of template dtype
description: basis_value_like at crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs:7031 hardcodes F64 for both scalar arm (Value::scalar_f64(1.0)) and tensor arm (DType::F64 + Literal::from_f64 elements) regardless of the template's dtype. Used in jacobian_jaxpr (line 7138) to construct unit-basis tangents for JVP-based Jacobian assembly. Although the final Jacobian flat is f64 anyway, F32 inputs trigger F64-widening JVP intermediates internally. Use one_literal_for_dtype + dtype_for_literal / template dtype to preserve precision.
notes: basis_value_like now uses one_literal_for_dtype(template_dtype) and zero_literal_for_dtype(template_dtype) so the unit-basis tangent preserves the template's dtype. F32 primals no longer trigger F64-widening JVP intermediates during Jacobian assembly. fj-ad 287 + ad_numerical_verification 75 pass, clippy clean.

title: Expose JAX config context helpers in fj-py

title: [parity-gap] safe egraph trig identities erase NaN boundaries
description: Review repro: public strict egraph dispatch now uses fj-egraph safe rules, but safe_algebraic_rules still contains exact identities such as sin^2+cos^2=>1, cosh^2-sinh^2=>1, and logistic(x)+logistic(-x)=>1. For inputs like x=+Inf or NaN, JAX/Rust evaluation of the original program observes NaN, while safe egraph extraction can choose constant 1. Repro: build a Jaxpr for add(mul(sin(x),sin(x)),mul(cos(x),cos(x))), run compose(..., vec![Transform::Jit]).with_mode(Strict).with_egraph_optimization(true).call([Value::scalar_f64(f64::INFINITY)]); expected NaN boundary preserved, observed risk: Some(1.0) after rewrite.
close_reason: Duplicate of frankenjax-hbam, already claimed by cod_223

title: Fuse non-inverse Transpose∘Transpose pairs in egraph shape-chain prepass
description: optimize_shape_parametric_chains currently cancels Transpose∘Transpose only when the pair is inverse (composed permutation is identity). Non-inverse Transpose∘Transpose pairs on a single-use intermediate could be fused into one Transpose carrying the composed permutation, halving the equation count and reducing downstream egraph saturation work. Composition: composed[j] = perm_b[perm_a[j]] where perm_b is the previous (innermost) transpose and perm_a is the current (outermost). Acceptance: (a) extend fuses_shape_chain with (Transpose, Transpose) and add a permutation-composition path in the fuse branch that rewrites current's permutation param and rewires current's input atom to previous's input atom; (b) only fuse when BOTH transposes carry an explicit permutation param (skip when either is the implicit reverse-axes form to keep the change conservative); (c) add unit tests for the (rank-3 non-inverse) fuse case and the (one-side-reverse) skip case in fj-egraph; (d) extend the existing reshape-chain proptest into a generic shape-chain proptest that includes Transpose mixed with Reshape (or add a sister proptest); (e) workspace tests + clippy clean via rch.
close_reason: Implemented Transpose∘Transpose fusion: new compose_explicit_transpose_perms returns Some(composed) iff both sides expose explicit permutation params; extended fuses_shape_chain with (Transpose, Transpose) gated on composability; the fuse branch rewrites current's permutation param before rewiring input+pushing. Implicit reverse-axes form conservatively skipped. Added 2 unit tests (positive rank-3 fuse with eval-equivalence + conservative skip when reverse). Composition semantics: composed[j] = perm_b[perm_a[j]]. Verified: fj-egraph 132 tests, conformance preserves_semantics 33 tests, clippy clean. Committed 2930c22.

title: fj-ad Cumprod VJP zeros complex gradients via as_f64 round-trip
description: crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs:2382-2468 Cumprod VJP collects gradient/primal elements via .as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0), computes forward cumprod and reverse-cumsum suffix in f64, then rebuilds with Literal::from_f64 and DType::F64. Same dtype-loss + silent-zero pattern as Cumsum VJP (frankenjax-o75a). Cumprod IS valid for complex inputs (complex multiplication is well-defined), so this is a real correctness bug. Acceptance: (a) preserve gradient/primal dtype throughout; (b) implement complex regime using (re, im) pair arithmetic — complex multiplication a*b = (a.re*b.re - a.im*b.im, a.re*b.im + a.im*b.re) for forward cumprod; complex division a/b = a * conj(b) / |b|^2 for the suffix_sum/x_i step; (c) preserve real-input dtype (F32/F64/etc) without widening to F64; (d) regression test for Complex64 input verifying Cumprod VJP keeps dtype and produces correct (non-zero) gradient; (e) workspace tests + clippy clean.
close_reason: Cumprod VJP now preserves dtype via dual regimes: complex inputs use (re,im) pair arithmetic with complex multiplication for forward cumprod and complex division (a+bi)(c-di)/(c^2+d^2) for the suffix/x_i step, with |x|^2>EPSILON guard mirroring existing real-divide guard; real inputs keep f64 reverse-suffix but re-encode at input's natural dtype (F32/BF16/F16/F64/I32/I64/U32/U64); Bool falls back to F64 per AD convention. Regression test pins x=[(2,0),(3,0)], g=[(1,0),(1,0)] → grad_x=[(4,0),(2,0)] with Complex64 dtype. fj-ad 281 tests pass; clippy clean. Committed 52f3d5e.

Scoreboard's 5ms p95 reflects a larger workload than the current bench corpus. Accepted levers under the shape-aware-rewrite-expansion umbrella: peer's prune_dead_single_output_equations DCE (272258e), my Reshape∘Reshape (kfvp), Transpose∘Transpose (r15o), Rev∘Rev (gdl2) fusion branches, and reshape-chain proptest (t3tl). Each lever has per-bead isomorphism proof + 33-test conformance preservation. No further generic saturation lever justified by current evidence; monitor only until the scoreboard workload is reproduced. Artifact: artifacts/performance/egraph_saturation_profile_2026-05-11.json. title: Add proptest fuzzing for shape-chain prepass over random op chains
description: Build on the existing optimize_shape_parametric_chains coverage by adding a property-based test in fj-egraph (proptest) that generates random chains of length 2-5 of shape-parametric ops (Transpose, Rev, ExpandDims, Squeeze, Reshape) on random small tensors and asserts that optimize_jaxpr produces a Jaxpr whose eval result equals the unoptimized eval result for every generated input. This stress-tests the cancellation, identity-elision, and Reshape-fusion branches of the prepass simultaneously and would catch regressions in any of those branches under cross-op interactions. Acceptance: (a) a new #[cfg(test)] proptest in crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs (or a new file under tests/) using existing fj-core/fj-interpreters dependencies; (b) shrinker produces minimal failing chains when broken; (c) at least 64 cases per test; (d) workspace tests + clippy clean via rch. NOTE: the test must keep tensor element counts consistent across the chain (e.g., Reshape preserves count, Transpose preserves count, Squeeze removes only size-1 dims, ExpandDims inserts size-1, Rev preserves count) so that randomly generated chains always produce a valid program.
close_reason: Implemented: added metamorphic_reshape_chain_prepass_preserves_semantics proptest in fj-egraph proptest_tests module. Strategy generates random chains of length 2..=5 of Reshape equations on tensors with element counts {1,2,4,6,8,12} and bounded rank-1/2/3 factorizations. For each chain it eval-equates the original vs optimize_jaxpr output and additionally asserts the optimized program collapses to a single Reshape, proving the (Reshape,Reshape) fusion branch from frankenjax-kfvp folds arbitrary-length single-use chains. Verified: fj-egraph 130 tests pass; clippy clean. Committed 1fa5fc8.

title: [code-review] security fuzz inventory omits cargo-fuzz targets
description: P1 finding from empty-backlog /testing-fuzzing rotation. crates/fj-conformance/fuzz/Cargo.toml declared cargo-fuzz binaries that build_security_adversarial_report did not represent as SecurityFuzzFamily entries, so security_gate_covers_every_manifest_fuzz_target failed before it could prove manifest coverage. The ledger_deserializer target also had no dedicated deterministic seed corpus or ledger-specific adversarial row binding. Repro before the fix: run `rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-conformance --test security_gate security_gate_covers_every_manifest_fuzz_target -- --nocapture`; it panics with `missing cargo-fuzz manifest target egraph_rewrite_rules`, and subsequent manifest-only targets are uncovered. Fix shipped in 446c87a: add FuzzSpec coverage for the missing fuzz binaries, add seed corpora for ledger_deserializer/proc_status_parser/jaxpr_serde_roundtrip/fft_roundtrip, and bind the evidence-ledger recovery row to ff_ledger_deserializer.

title: Add nextafter invertive metamorphic coverage
description: testing-metamorphic Phase A gap: crates/fj-conformance/tests/nextafter_oracle.rs covers scalar/tensor examples and dtype preservation, but lacks an invertive bit-level MR for one-ULP stepping. Candidate MR matrix: nextafter(nextafter(x,+inf),-inf)==x for finite interior x scores 5 sensitivity x 4 independence / 1 cost = 20; tensor elementwise roundtrip scores 4 x 4 / 1 = 16; same-direction monotonicity scores 3 x 3 / 1 = 9. Scope: add focused f64 and f32 scalar/tensor metamorphic tests in the existing nextafter oracle file proving upward/downward one-step roundtrip returns original bits for finite non-boundary values, while preserving existing dtype/shape checks. Validate with RCH cargo test -p fj-conformance --test nextafter_oracle plus scoped check/clippy.

title: Default vmap pad interior padding in BatchTrace

close_reason: Defaulted omitted BatchTrace pad interior padding to zero and added vmap regression coverage.

title: Bound batched fixture timeout thread fan-out
description: The fj-conformance batched transform fixture runner in crates/fj-conformance/src/lib.rs currently clones the entire bundle, spawns one OS thread per case up front, and then waits on each receiver with a timeout. Large oracle bundles can therefore create hundreds of threads immediately, and timed-out cases keep running in detached background threads. Change run_transform_fixture_bundle_batched so it starts each case only when it is being waited on and joins successful case threads after receiving their result, preserving report order while preventing all cases from fanning out at once. Add regression coverage with multiple delayed cases to prove timeout reporting still preserves case order. Validate with rch cargo test -p fj-conformance test_batch_runner, rch cargo check -p fj-conformance --all-targets, rch cargo clippy -p fj-conformance --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check, and ubs on the touched file.
close_reason: Changed the batched transform fixture runner to start each case only when waiting on it, join completed worker threads, retain timeout reporting order, and added delayed-plus-fast regression coverage. Validated fmt, UBS, targeted fj-conformance test, fj-conformance check/clippy, and full workspace tests via rch.

That leaves production crates such as fj-dispatch, fj-trace, fj-cache, fj-api, fj-ledger, fj-interpreters, fj-runtime, fj-egraph, fj-backend-cpu, and fj-conformance unchecked even though they contain the transform ledger, public API, cache, and conformance harness surfaces. Fix by expanding the no-stub source-marker/default-return guard to every production src crate while excluding test-only helper names and intentional fixture text. Validate with rch cargo test -p fj-conformance --test no_stub_regression.

Example remote test evidence: expected polygamma(1, 1)=pi^2/6=1.6449340668482264, current fj-lax returned 1.644687650701086; expected polygamma(2, 1)=-2*zeta(3)=-2.4041138063191885, current returned -2.404006410193136. Improve polygamma_approx/asymptotic accuracy enough for tight upstream-oracle tolerances, then tighten crates/fj-conformance/tests/polygamma_oracle.rs from POLYGAMMA_APPROX_TOL=5e-4 toward 1e-6.

title: fj-ad dynamic_update_slice_vjp force-converts all gradients to F64 + zeroes complex via as_f64()
description: crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs:3494-3589 dynamic_update_slice_vjp iterates the gradient tensor through f64 () and rebuilds output tensors as DType::F64. Same dtype-loss + complex-silent-zero pattern as scan_vjp (frankenjax-kpqu): Complex64/Complex128 inputs yield all-zero F64 gradients; F32 inputs widen to F64. Acceptance: (a) refactor the inner iteration to copy Literal values directly without lossy f64 round-trip (use the dtype-aware zero literal from zero_literal_for_dtype where 'zeroing' the update region is needed); (b) build the output TensorValue with the input's dtype; (c) update the scalar-fallback arms to use zeros_like (which now preserves dtype after o0mj) for the update / start-index gradients; (d) add a regression test that runs dynamic_update_slice_vjp with a Complex64 operand and verifies the resulting gradients (a) are Complex64-dtyped and (b) are not identically zero; (e) workspace tests + clippy clean.
close_reason: Fix already landed at commit 40957b3 by peer cc-frankenjax with the same approach: dynamic_update_slice_vjp now copies Literal values verbatim through iteration, uses zero_literal_for_dtype for zeroed update region, and builds output tensors with g_tensor.dtype instead of hard-coded F64. My local commit was subsumed by the upstream peer commit during autostash-rebase. fj-ad 275 + ad_numerical_verification 63 pass; clippy clean.

title: [code-review] Safe egraph mode still applies log-exp overflow rewrite
description: Review finding from recent numerical-safety-mode commit: OptimizationConfig::safe() disables several domain-boundary rewrites, but log(exp(x)) -> x remains in the safe rule set. For large finite x, exp(x) overflows to Inf and log(Inf) is Inf, so rewriting to x changes observable IEEE/JAX-style behavior. Move log-exp into the numerically unsafe rule set and cover safe/aggressive behavior with a regression test.
close_reason: Moved log-exp into unsafe egraph rules and added overflow-boundary safe/aggressive regression coverage.

title: Strengthen fj-ad hfry OneHot/Argsort VJP tests with validate_dtype_consistency
description: The hfry regression tests assert grad.shape and grad.dtype match the input but don't check that every element literal kind matches the declared dtype. Add .validate_dtype_consistency() calls to make those tests pin the full invariant (shape + dtype + element kinds) like go38/n43l.
notes: Strengthened OneHot/Argsort VJP regression tests with .validate_dtype_consistency() calls on the produced cotangent so every element literal is checked against the declared dtype, not just shape and declared-dtype equality. fj-ad 287 pass, clippy clean.

title: fj-ad Gather VJP hardcodes F64 zero operand for scatter, widening complex/non-F64 gradients
description: crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs:1959-1990 Gather VJP builds a zero operand tensor with hardcoded DType::F64 and Literal::from_f64(0.0) regardless of the gradient/operand dtype, then scatters the gradient onto it. For Complex64/Complex128 operand gradients, this widens the gradient to F64 (or fails if scatter's strict dtype matching rejects the mismatch). For F32/BF16/F16, it also widens. Acceptance: (a) detect the gradient's dtype from g (use g_tensor.dtype for tensor g or dtype_for_literal for scalar g); (b) create the zero_operand tensor with that dtype using zero_literal_for_dtype; (c) regression test for Complex64 input verifying Gather VJP returns a Complex64-typed operand gradient; (d) workspace tests + clippy clean.
close_reason: Gather VJP zero-operand now picks dtype from g (g_tensor.dtype for tensor g, dtype_for_literal for scalar g). Zero tensor built with zero_literal_for_dtype instead of Literal::from_f64(0.0); TensorValue dtype = g_dtype instead of hardcoded F64. Helper-invariant regression test guards zero_literal_for_dtype + dtype_for_literal which the fix relies on. fj-ad 280 tests pass; clippy clean. Committed 2990980.

title: Fix fj-lax conv ops silently zeroing Complex/Bool inputs via as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0)
description: eval_conv_1d/2d at crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_ops.rs:3295-3296 and 3407-3408 use as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0) — but as_f64() returns None for Bool/Complex64/Complex128 (fj-core/src/lib.rs:557), so complex or bool conv inputs silently produce zeros instead of an error. JAX conv is float-only; we should reject these dtypes explicitly with TypeMismatch.
notes: Not a real bug — eval_conv at crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_ops.rs:3128-3138 gates lhs.dtype and rhs.dtype to floating dtypes (BF16/F16/F32/F64) before dispatching to eval_conv_1d/eval_conv_2d. So the as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0) calls at lines 3295/3296/3407/3408 can never see Complex/Bool literals. The unwrap_or is defensive but unreachable. No code change required.

Add log_vjp_numerical_complex64 asserting Complex64 dtype + values within 1e-5 f32 tolerance. Workspace tests + clippy clean.

title: [parity-gap] public egraph cbrt triple rewrite is algebraically wrong
description: Review finding from commits 9c21ee1 and recent egraph safety work: public strict dispatch now routes egraph_optimize=true through the safe optimizer, but safe_algebraic_rules still contains cbrt(cbrt(cbrt(x))) => cbrt(x). That identity is false for ordinary finite inputs: x=64 evaluates to cbrt(cbrt(4.0)) in the original program, while the rewrite returns 4.0. Repro: compose a Jaxpr with three Cbrt equations under Transform::Jit, enable with_egraph_optimization(true), and call with Value::scalar_f64(64.0). Expected strict result is nested cbrt semantics, not cbrt(x). Remove the invalid rewrite and add public strict egraph conformance coverage.
close_reason: Removed the invalid cbrt(cbrt(cbrt(x))) rewrite, replaced the old rule-shape assertion with semantic preservation coverage, and added public strict egraph API conformance coverage.

title: [reality-check] support jax.checkpoint/remat for memory-efficient AD
description:
JAX's checkpoint (aka remat) enables memory-efficient gradient computation by recomputing forward-pass intermediates during the backward pass instead of storing them.

**Gap:** FrankenJAX tape-based AD stores all intermediates. For large models, this limits batch size.

**Effort:** Medium - requires modifying fj-ad tape structure to support checkpoint boundaries.

**Prerequisites:** None (works on CPU).

**JAX reference:** `jax.checkpoint`, `jax.remat`
close_reason: Implemented: checkpoint() wrapper in fj-api with grad_jaxpr_with_cotangent() helper. Re-runs forward during backward for memory efficiency. 3 tests verify semantics: forward preservation, gradient correctness, memory savings reporting. See commits 4911b5d, 36326ce.

title: [code-review] safe egraph complex rewrites skip dtype validation
description:
Review evidence from the six-hour egraph hardening window:
- `crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs` still keeps `real-complex`, `imag-complex`, and `complex-real-imag` in `safe_algebraic_rules` at lines around 436-438.
- `fj-lax` `eval_real`/`eval_imag` reject non-complex inputs, and `eval_complex` validates part dtypes/shapes before constructing a complex value.
- Safe egraph extraction can remove those validation operations and return an input variable directly.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add a regression in `crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs` or `crates/fj-conformance/tests/egraph_preserves_semantics.rs` that builds `out = Complex(Real(z), Imag(z))` with one input `z`.
2. Evaluate the original with `args = [Value::scalar_f64(3.0)]`. Expected under the current fj-lax contract: `eval_jaxpr` returns the existing `TypeMismatch` from `Real`/`Imag` because the input is not complex.
3. Evaluate `optimize_jaxpr_with_config(&jaxpr, &OptimizationConfig::safe())` and `optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr)` with the same args. Current safe/default can rewrite to just `z` and return `Value::scalar_f64(3.0)`, converting an input validation error into success.
4. Add a second case for `Real(Complex(r, i))` with `r = Value::scalar_f64(1.0)` and `i = Value::scalar_bool(true)`. Original `Complex` should reject the bool part; safe/default can rewrite to `r`.
5. Run: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_complex_safe_rules RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph complex_validation -- --nocapture`.

Suggested fix: move the complex safe rewrites to `numerically_unsafe_rules` or a typed/validated lowering phase that proves complex input and part validity before eliminating operations.
close_reason: Moved complex validation-skipping egraph rewrites out of safe mode and added validation regression coverage.

title: Broaden no_stub_regression REPRESENTATIVE_DTYPES to include BF16/F16/F32/I32/Complex64
description: crates/fj-conformance/tests/no_stub_regression.rs:27's REPRESENTATIVE_DTYPES sweeps only I64/U32/U64/F64/Complex128/Bool — 6 of 11 DType variants. Missing BF16, F16, F32, I32, Complex64. If a primitive produces a banned substring ("not yet implemented", "placeholder", etc.) specifically for one of those untested dtypes, the regression test would silently miss it. Add the missing variants so the per-(primitive × dtype) sweep is exhaustive.
notes: Expanded REPRESENTATIVE_DTYPES in no_stub_regression.rs to cover all 11 DType variants (was 6). Added scalar input arms for I32 (shares Literal::I64), BF16, F16, F32, Complex64. title: fj-cache FileCache::evict and clear() silently swallow filesystem errors
description: Follow-up to frankenjax-xda6. crates/fj-cache/src/backend.rs FileCache::evict at line 203-206 uses 'std::fs::remove_file(&path).is_ok()' which conflates 'file did not exist' (returns false per trait doc) with 'file existed but could not be removed' (e.g., permission denied — also returns false). Same pattern in clear() (lines 189-197) silently discards remove_file errors during the per-entry loop. Acceptance: (a) add 'evict_failures: AtomicU64' and 'clear_failures: AtomicU64' fields to FileCache; (b) increment evict_failures only when remove_file returns an error other than NotFound (matching the trait's 'did not exist' semantics); (c) increment clear_failures when read_dir fails OR when any per-entry remove_file fails; (d) expose via 'pub fn evict_failure_count(&self) -> u64' and 'pub fn clear_failure_count(&self) -> u64'; (e) add unit tests that exercise both failure paths (e.g., evict against non-existent cache_dir parent for clear_failures, and a read-only file scenario if feasible); (f) workspace tests + clippy clean via rch.
close_reason: Extended silent-failure-counter pattern from put() to evict() and clear(). evict() now distinguishes NotFound (normal false return) from other IO errors via ErrorKind. clear() now records read_dir whole-dir failures and per-entry remove failures. Added evict_failure_count() + clear_failure_count() non-trait accessors. 4 new tests (missing/success cases for both). fj-cache 85 tests pass; clippy clean. Committed 2e6f8be.

title: fj-cache FileCache::put silently swallows rename failures
description: crates/fj-cache/src/backend.rs:150-164 implements atomic write via temp-file + rename. The rename step uses 'let _ = std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, &path)' which silently discards rename errors (disk-full, permission, cross-device link, concurrent removal of parent dir). When rename fails after the temp write succeeded, the cache silently fails to store the artifact and the caller has no way to detect it. The trait signature 'fn put(&mut self, ...)' returns () so we cannot propagate the error directly without breaking callers. Acceptance: (a) add a 'put_failures: AtomicU64' counter field to FileCache; (b) increment the counter on either std::fs::write failure or std::fs::rename failure inside put(); (c) expose the count via a new method 'pub fn put_failure_count(&self) -> u64' on FileCache (non-trait, non-breaking); (d) add a unit test that triggers a put_failure by attempting to put under a path whose parent directory has been removed mid-flight, asserting the counter increments and downstream stats() entry_count stays unchanged; (e) workspace tests + clippy clean via rch.
close_reason: Added put_failures AtomicU64 + put_failure_count() accessor to FileCache. Replaced let-underscore-discard branches in put() with explicit match that increments counter on temp-write OR rename failure, with best-effort cleanup. Added 2 unit tests (NotFound parent dir bumps counter; healthy put keeps it 0). Verified: fj-cache 81 tests pass; clippy clean. Committed 9992982.

title: Harden BufferView slice range overflow
description: Multi-pass bug hunt found crates/fj-runtime/src/buffer.rs BufferView::slice computes offset + len before validating bounds. For adversarial or saturated usize inputs this can panic in debug builds due integer overflow, and in optimized builds can wrap before the subsequent slice expression panics or misclassifies the range. Replace the addition with checked_add so out-of-range or overflowing slices return None as documented. Add a regression test using usize::MAX to prove BufferView::slice is panic-free and returns None for overflowing ranges. Validate with rch cargo test -p fj-runtime, rch cargo check -p fj-runtime --all-targets, rch cargo clippy -p fj-runtime --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check, and ubs on the touched file.
close_reason: Hardened BufferView::slice with checked_add so overflowing offset+len returns None without panic; regression test covers usize::MAX overflow; workspace fmt/check/clippy/test pass on current HEAD.

title: ReduceWindow same padding is offset incorrectly
description: eval_reduce_window computes SAME output length with ceil(input/stride) but starts every window at out_index * stride, so padding is effectively only on the high side. JAX/XLA SAME semantics split total padding with low padding floor(total/2), so examples like input [1,2,3,4,5], window=3, stride=1 should produce [3,6,9,12,9] instead of [6,9,12,9,5]. Implement low-padding offsets for SAME in rank-N reduce_window and add regression coverage.
close_reason: Implemented centered SAME padding offsets for reduce_window and added rank-1/rank-2 regressions

title: [modes-of-reasoning] numerical-stability-vs-JIT-correctness
description:
E-graph rewrites (exp-log, div-self, reassociation) are mathematically valid but numerically treacherous near domain boundaries. Eager vs JIT diverge on edge cases (0, denormal, NaN, Inf). Conformance detects drift post-hoc but cannot prevent unsafe rewrites.

Key locations:
- fj-egraph/src/lib.rs:269-324 (rewrite rules without domain guards)
- fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs:219-222 (underflow-prone complex reciprocal)
- fj-conformance/src/numerical_stability.rs:857-920 (tolerance enforcement gap)

Suggested work:
1. Add domain predicates to e-graph rules (div-self only when provably non-zero)
2. Implement numerical safety mode that disables associativity rewrites
3. Extend conformance gate to compare JIT vs eager on edge-case inputs
close_reason: Added OptimizationConfig with numerical_safety_mode flag in ceea42f. Safe mode disables 6 unsafe rewrites: exp-log, div-self, mul-reciprocal, div-mul-cancel, log-product, log-quotient.

title: fj-ad Pad VJP zeros complex gradients and widens real inputs to F64
description: crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs:2053-2150 Pad VJP extracts operand gradient elements via 'g_tensor.elements[flat].as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0)' and rebuilds as DType::F64. Same silent-zero + widening pattern as Cumsum/scan/dynamic_update_slice VJPs. Complex inputs become all-zero F64; real inputs (F32/BF16/F16) widen to F64. Pad VJP also returns 'Value::scalar_f64(g_total_sum - op_grad_sum)' for pad_value gradient — wrong dtype for complex inputs. Acceptance: (a) preserve dtype for operand gradient by copying Literal values verbatim when the position is in-range; use zero_literal_for_dtype for out-of-range positions; (b) build operand gradient with input's dtype, not hardcoded F64; (c) compute pad_value gradient with dtype-aware accumulator (complex: (re,im) pair, real: f64 then re-encoded to input dtype); (d) regression test for Complex64 input verifying operand gradient stays Complex64 and not all-zero; (e) workspace tests + clippy clean.
close_reason: Pad VJP now preserves gradient dtype: operand gradient copies Literal values verbatim with zero_literal_for_dtype for out-of-range positions, output dtype = g_tensor.dtype. Pad-value gradient tracks operand-feeding g positions via BTreeSet, then sums remaining (padding) positions in dtype-appropriate domain ((re,im) pair for Complex; f64 for real with re-encode to input dtype). Scalar fallback uses zeros_like(g) for pad_value. New regression test pins Complex64 operand grad correctness + Complex64 pad_value sum = (16, 17). fj-ad 278 tests pass; clippy clean. Committed 127616e.

title: Add Cummax/Cummin conformance oracle coverage
description: Parity gap discovered by diffing implemented FrankenJAX primitives against upstream JAX lax cumulative reductions: upstream exposes lax.cummax and lax.cummin, and fj-lax implements Primitive::Cummax/Cummin, but fj-conformance cumulative_oracle.rs only covers cumsum/cumprod. Add focused oracle coverage for 1-D, axis, reverse, duplicate extrema, and dtype-preserving behavior.
